nodes.name: Create LinkedIn Posts

values.content:
=Using the news item details below:

Headline: {{ $json.headline }}
Summary: {{ $json.summary }}

Craft a concise, non-promotional LinkedIn post in a smart, deadpan style with subtle humor. Focus on clearly conveying the main points and insights so readers gain practical value. 
- Break up the text into short paragraphs or bullet points for clarity.
- Use line breaks where helpful.
- End with an observation or question that encourages reflection—without being overly salesy or flashy.
- Keep it under 80 words total.

nodes.name: LinkedIn
nodes.notes: This linkedIn node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
# Workflow Overview

**Name:** Transform Gmail Newsletters into Insightful LinkedIn Posts Using OpenAI

**Purpose:**  
- **Filter Newsletters:** Use the Gmail node to process emails from a specific sender (e.g., `newsletter@example.com`).  
- **Extract Key Items:** Leverage an OpenAI node to identify and summarize the top news items from each newsletter.  
- **Generate Posts:** Automatically create concise, informative, and subtly humorous LinkedIn posts for each news item.  
- **Publish:** Post the refined content to your LinkedIn account with the LinkedIn node.

**Setup Steps:**  
1. **Gmail Node:** Configure and rename to "Filter Gmail Newsletter" with the appropriate sender filter.  
2. **OpenAI Nodes:** Ensure API credentials are set; customize prompt texts if desired.  
3. **LinkedIn Node:** Rename to "Post to LinkedIn" and verify correct OAuth2 credentials.

**Customization Tips:**  
- Modify the OpenAI prompts to fine-tune the tone and structure of the LinkedIn posts.  
- Add additional formatting (e.g., Function nodes) for post readability if needed.

*This workflow turns your regular newsletters into engaging, ready-to-share LinkedIn insights in just a few simple steps!*

parameters.content:
# Workflow Overview

**Name:** Transform Gmail Newsletters into Insightful LinkedIn Posts Using OpenAI

**Purpose:**  
- **Filter Newsletters:** Use the Gmail node to process emails from a specific sender (e.g., `newsletter@example.com`).  
- **Extract Key Items:** Leverage an OpenAI node to identify and summarize the top news items from each newsletter.  
- **Generate Posts:** Automatically create concise, informative, and subtly humorous LinkedIn posts for each news item.  
- **Publish:** Post the refined content to your LinkedIn account with the LinkedIn node.

**Setup Steps:**  
1. **Gmail Node:** Configure and rename to "Filter Gmail Newsletter" with the appropriate sender filter.  
2. **OpenAI Nodes:** Ensure API credentials are set; customize prompt texts if desired.  
3. **LinkedIn Node:** Rename to "Post to LinkedIn" and verify correct OAuth2 credentials.

**Customization Tips:**  
- Modify the OpenAI prompts to fine-tune the tone and structure of the LinkedIn posts.  
- Add additional formatting (e.g., Function nodes) for post readability if needed.

*This workflow turns your regular newsletters into engaging, ready-to-share LinkedIn insights in just a few simple steps!*

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
linked In  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "Gmail" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Extract News Items" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create LinkedIn Posts" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "LinkedIn" node (n8n-nodes-base.linkedIn) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
